pattern: A pile size is a losing position exactly when every perfect-square removal leaves the opponent a winning position, so the whole game folds into a single forward dynamic-programming pass over pile sizes.
hints: A position with n stones is winning for the player to move if there exists SOME perfect square k*k <= n that leaves the opponent in a losing position.
Build a boolean table dp[0..n] bottom-up: dp[0] is a loss for the player to move (no stones left to take); dp[i] is a win if dp[i - k*k] is a loss for some k with k*k <= i.
Precompute the list of perfect squares up to n once, then for each i scan that list and stop as soon as one square leaves a losing position for the opponent.

edge_inputs.description: requires looking two moves ahead, not just checking if n or n minus a square is a perfect square
large non-square value one below the maximum

stress.description: every pile size from 1 up to the maximum, exercised via random sampling
